test_cases.name: happy_path_standard_input
test_cases.scenario: User provides detailed sleep habits with clear problems and constraints; skill generates complete protocol with environmental and behavioral recommendations.

input.sleep_habits_description: I go to bed around 11 PM and wake at 6 AM, but it takes me 45 minutes to fall asleep. My bedroom is warm (around 72°F), has street light coming through the window, and my partner snores. I drink 2-3 cups of coffee daily, last one around 4 PM. I work a desk job and exercise only on weekends. I feel tired during the day despite 7 hours in bed. My main complaint is the long time to fall asleep and waking 2-3 times per night due to noise.
input.medical_context: No diagnosed sleep disorders; occasional stress from work.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill executes all 7 phases successfully. Generates sleep profile with 6 dimensions scored, identifies 4-6 problems (long sleep onset, nighttime awakenings, warm room, light, caffeine, noise), produces 3-5 environmental recommendations (temperature, light, noise control), 3-5 behavioral recommendations (caffeine cutoff, consistent schedule), phased 4-week implementation plan with success metrics, and comprehensive 2500-3500 word protocol document.

test_cases.name: happy_path_minimal_input
test_cases.scenario: User provides minimal but sufficient sleep description (100+ characters, 3+ data points); skill infers constraints and generates protocol with reasonable defaults.

input.sleep_habits_description: I sleep from midnight to 7 AM but wake up 4-5 times per night. My bedroom is noisy because I live near a highway. I drink coffee until 6 PM. I'm stressed about work. I want to sleep through the night.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill parses minimal input, flags missing information (budget, flexibility, goals) in clarification_flags, infers reasonable defaults (medium budget, moderate flexibility, goal = sleep continuity), generates complete protocol with emphasis on noise and caffeine interventions, includes note that some recommendations are based on inferred constraints.

test_cases.name: edge_case_very_low_budget
test_cases.scenario: User has very low budget (low budget_level, <$100 total) and low flexibility (flexibility_score = 3); skill generates minimal protocol with only free or very cheap interventions.

input.sleep_habits_description: I go to bed at 11 PM, wake at 6 AM, but it takes 30 minutes to fall asleep. My bedroom is warm and bright. I can't afford expensive changes. I work long hours and can't change my schedule much. I drink coffee until 5 PM.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies budget and flexibility constraints, generates minimal 1-2 phase protocol with only free or <$20 interventions (caffeine cutoff, blackout curtains from cardboard, earplugs, consistent bedtime). Includes explicit note: 'You indicated low willingness to change habits and limited budget. This minimal protocol addresses only the highest-impact, easiest changes. Results will be limited without additional behavior changes.' Avoids expensive recommendations (new mattress, thermostat, soundproofing).

test_cases.name: edge_case_shared_bedroom_constraints
test_cases.scenario: User lives in shared bedroom with partner; skill generates recommendations that don't disturb partner (earplugs instead of white noise, personal cooling pad instead of room temperature change).

input.sleep_habits_description: I share a bedroom with my partner. I go to bed at 11 PM but take 40 minutes to fall asleep. The room is warm and my partner snores loudly. I can't change the room temperature or use loud white noise because my partner needs quiet and prefers warmth. I drink 2 cups of coffee daily, last one at 3 PM. I exercise 3 times per week in the morning.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies shared bedroom constraint, generates recommendations that don't affect partner: earplugs or sleep mask instead of white noise, personal cooling pad or lighter blanket instead of room temperature change, blackout curtains (if partner agrees), caffeine cutoff, consistent schedule. Includes explicit note in personalization_notes: 'You share a bedroom. We prioritized recommendations that don't disturb your partner: earplugs instead of white noise, personal cooling pad instead of room temperature change.' Avoids soundproofing, room temperature changes, or loud interventions.

test_cases.name: edge_case_no_problems_optimization_mode
test_cases.scenario: User reports sleeping well on all dimensions but wants optimization; skill shifts to enhancement mode and generates recommendations for peak performance.

input.sleep_habits_description: I sleep from 10:30 PM to 6:30 AM, fall asleep within 10 minutes, sleep through the night, and feel well-rested. My bedroom is cool (68°F), dark, and quiet. I exercise daily and don't drink caffeine after 2 PM. I'm happy with my sleep but want to optimize for peak daytime performance and mental clarity.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies that user has no sleep problems (all dimensions score 8-10), shifts to optimization mode. Generates minimal problem_analysis (empty or 'no problems identified'), focuses on enhancement recommendations: advanced sleep tracking, meditation for stress resilience, nap optimization (20-30 min in early afternoon), light exposure timing for circadian optimization, recovery-focused exercise timing. Includes note: 'Your sleep is already excellent. This protocol focuses on optimization and enhancement for peak daytime performance.' Avoids problem-focused language.

test_cases.name: error_handling_vague_description
test_cases.scenario: User provides vague sleep description (<100 characters, <3 data points); skill triggers Phase 1 error handling and requests elaboration.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill detects that sleep_habits_description is too vague (only 3 words, <100 characters, <3 data points). Triggers Phase 1 error handling (Adjust response). Returns error message: 'Description is too vague to proceed. Please provide more detail. Answer these questions: (1) What time do you currently go to bed and wake up? (2) How long does it take you to fall asleep? (3) How many times do you wake during the night? (4) What do you think is causing the problem? (5) Describe your bedroom (temperature, light, noise, bed quality).' Does not proceed to Phase 2.

test_cases.name: error_handling_medical_red_flag
test_cases.scenario: User reports severe sleep apnea symptoms or insomnia >3 months; skill flags medical referral and includes prominent disclaimer in protocol.

input.sleep_habits_description: I've had insomnia for 6 months. I go to bed at 11 PM but can't fall asleep until 2-3 AM. I wake 5-6 times per night. My partner says I snore loudly and sometimes stop breathing. I'm exhausted during the day. I've tried everything but nothing helps.
input.medical_context: No formal diagnosis but suspect sleep apnea. Taking no medications.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies red flags: insomnia >3 months, suspected sleep apnea (snoring, breathing pauses, daytime sleepiness). Adds prominent disclaimer in problem_analysis and protocol_document: 'Your symptoms suggest sleep apnea, which requires medical evaluation and treatment. Sleep hygiene alone may not be sufficient. Please consult a sleep specialist or physician before implementing this protocol. Mention your symptoms: loud snoring, witnessed breathing pauses, daytime sleepiness, insomnia >6 months.' Includes medical_referral_flags array with: ['Insomnia lasting >6 months', 'Suspected sleep apnea (snoring, breathing pauses, daytime sleepiness)']. Still generates protocol but emphasizes medical referral.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_boundary_feasibility_threshold
test_cases.scenario: User constraints are very limiting (low budget, low flexibility, shared bedroom, night shift work); skill filters interventions and ensures at least 3 feasible options remain (feasibility_score ≥5).

input.sleep_habits_description: I work night shift (11 PM to 7 AM) and sleep during the day (8 AM to 4 PM). I share a small bedroom with my roommate who works days. My bedroom is warm, bright, and noisy. I have a very tight budget (<$50 total). I can't change my work schedule. I drink 3 cups of coffee during my shift. I feel exhausted during work and sleep poorly during the day.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies extreme constraints: night shift (can't change schedule), low budget (<$50), shared bedroom, low flexibility (2/10). Filters interventions by feasibility_score ≥5. Remaining feasible interventions: (1) Earplugs ($5-10, feasibility 8), (2) Sleep mask or blackout curtains ($10-20, feasibility 8), (3) Caffeine cutoff 4 hours before sleep ($0, feasibility 6), (4) Consistent daytime sleep schedule ($0, feasibility 5). Includes constraint_notes: 'Night shift work limits schedule changes. We prioritized low-cost, non-disruptive interventions: earplugs, sleep mask, consistent daytime sleep schedule. Results may be limited without schedule change.' Ensures at least 3 feasible interventions remain (quality gate pass).
phase_coverage: Phase 3: Constraint and Feasibility Analysis (quality gate)

test_cases.name: quality_gate_boundary_recommendation_specificity
test_cases.scenario: Skill generates environmental and behavioral recommendations that are specific enough to execute without further clarification (includes specific targets, products, methods, timelines).

input.sleep_habits_description: I go to bed at 11 PM and wake at 7 AM but take 35 minutes to fall asleep. My bedroom is 70°F and I feel warm. I use my phone until 11 PM. I drink coffee at 4 PM. I want to fall asleep faster.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates recommendations with specific, actionable details: (1) 'Lower bedroom temperature to 65-68°F using a programmable thermostat or by opening windows' (NOT 'Make room cooler'). (2) 'Stop caffeine intake after 2 PM; switch to herbal tea or water' (NOT 'Reduce caffeine'). (3) 'Stop using phone 30 minutes before bed (10:30 PM); read a physical book instead' (NOT 'Reduce screen time'). Each recommendation includes: specific target (65-68°F, 2 PM, 30 min), method (thermostat, herbal tea, book), cost ($0-200), timeline (immediate/1-2 weeks), success indicator ('Fall asleep within 20 minutes'). No vague language like 'Make it better' or 'Try to reduce.' Quality gate: every recommendation is specific enough that user could execute without further clarification.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_boundary_protocol_document_completeness
test_cases.scenario: Skill generates comprehensive protocol document (2500-3500 words) with all required sections: executive summary, assessment, environmental changes, behavioral changes, implementation plan, tracking log, success metrics, troubleshooting, sleep science reference, personalization notes, professional help guidance.

input.sleep_habits_description: I go to bed at 11 PM, wake at 6:30 AM, but take 30 minutes to fall asleep and wake 2-3 times per night. My bedroom is 72°F, has street light, and my roommate snores. I drink 2 cups of coffee daily (last one at 4 PM). I exercise twice per week. I'm stressed about work. I want to sleep through the night and feel more rested.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates protocol_document string (2500-3500 words) with all required sections: (1) Executive Summary (200-300 words): user's profile, key problems, expected improvements, timeline, personalization note. (2) Your Sleep Assessment (300-400 words): scores for 6 dimensions in user-friendly language. (3) Environmental Changes (400-500 words): each recommendation with what/why/how/cost/timeline/success indicator. (4) Behavioral Changes (400-500 words): each recommendation with what/why/how/timeline/difficulty/success indicator. (5) Your 4-Week Implementation Plan (300-400 words): phased timeline with specific actions per week. (6) Daily Sleep Tracking Log (200 words + template): explanation and blank template. (7) Success Metrics & Checkpoints (200-300 words): targets and weekly review prompts. (8) Troubleshooting Guide (300-400 words): common obstacles and solutions. (9) Sleep Science Reference (300-400 words): why each recommendation works. (10) Personalization Notes (200-300 words): how recommendations were tailored to constraints. (11) When to Seek Professional Help (100-150 words): red flags and guidance. Document is formatted with clear headings, bullet points, tables, and is readable by non-expert. Quality gate: document is 2500-3500 words, includes all sections, is actionable and self-contained.
